post.description: Create a new application from a Git repository or Docker image. The application is created with a default web process, auto deploy disabled, and an initial deployment is not triggered automatically - use the Deploy endpoint to start the first deployment. Auto deploy can be enabled later via the Update endpoint but only works with privateGit applications.

display_name.description: Human-readable name for the new application. Must be between 2 and 64 characters.

cluster_id.description: Identifier of the cluster (data center location) where the application will be created.

source.description: Where the application source code comes from. `privateGit` - private Git repository connected via OAuth. `publicGit` - public Git repository URL. `dockerImage` - Docker container image from a registry.

project_id.description: Identifier of the project to group this application under. When omitted, the application is not assigned to any project.

git_type.description: Git hosting provider for the repository. `github` - GitHub. `bitbucket` - Bitbucket. `gitlab` - GitLab. Required for private git sources when the provider cannot be auto-detected.

repo_url.description: Full URL of the Git repository. Required when source is `privateGit` or `publicGit`.

default_branch.description: Git branch used for deployments. When omitted, the repository default branch is used.

docker_image.description: Docker image reference including tag or digest. Required when source is `dockerImage`.

docker_registry_credential_id.description: Identifier of a stored Docker registry credential for pulling private images. Only used when source is `dockerImage`.

company_id.description: Identifier of the company that owns this application

project_id.description: Identifier of the project this application is grouped under

name.description: Unique system name used in URLs and internal references. Auto-generated from the display name and cannot be changed after creation.

namespace.description: Kubernetes namespace where the application workloads run. Assigned automatically during creation.

get.description: Retrieve build and deployment logs for a specific deployment. These include output from the build process and rollout steps. Use the `filters` parameter for advanced filtering by severity, message content, tags, or attributes.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the time range in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to 1 hour ago if not provided.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the time range in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to now if not provided.

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: JSON-encoded array of filter objects for advanced log filtering. Each filter has a `key`, `operator`, and `value` or `values` field. Example: [{"key":"severity","operator":"EQUALS","value":"ERROR"}]

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of log entries to return (1-5000)

id.description: Unique identifier for the log entry

service.description: Service that produced the log entry, such as APP_RUNTIME, APP_ACCESS, APP_DEPLOYMENT, SS_DEPLOYMENT, or SS_ACCESS

severity.description: Log severity level such as INFO, WARN, ERROR, or DEBUG

timestamp.description: Formatted timestamp of the log entry

tags.description: Key-value pairs of tags associated with the log entry, such as container name or resource ID

attributes.description: Additional structured attributes attached to the log entry

unix_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp of the log entry in microseconds

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of all processes for an application, ordered by their display order and creation time. Each process includes its runtime configuration, scaling strategy, and resource limits.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items per page

app_id.description: Identifier of the application this process belongs to

key.description: Unique key identifying the process within the application, derived from the Procfile entry name

type.description: Process runtime type. `web` - HTTP server process that handles incoming web traffic. `worker` - background process that runs continuously. `cron` - scheduled process that runs at specified intervals. `job` - one-time process that runs to completion and then stops.

display_name.description: Human-readable name for the process shown in the dashboard

entrypoint.description: Command used to start the process

port.description: Port number the process listens on for incoming connections

is_ingress_enabled.description: Whether external traffic routing is enabled for this process. Only applicable to web processes.

ingress_protocol.description: Protocol used for ingress traffic. `http` - standard HTTP/HTTPS traffic. `grpc` - gRPC protocol. Only applicable to web processes.

type.description: Manual scaling type
Horizontal auto-scaling type

targetCpuPercent.description: Target CPU utilization percentage for autoscaling

targetMemoryPercent.description: Target memory utilization percentage for autoscaling

scaleUpIntervalSeconds.description: Cooldown period in seconds between scale-up events

scaleUpIncrement.description: Number of instances to add per scale-up event

scaleDownIntervalSeconds.description: Cooldown period in seconds between scale-down events

scaleDownIncrement.description: Number of instances to remove per scale-down event

resource_type_id.description: Identifier of the process resource type (machine size) assigned to this process. Use the List Process Resource Types endpoint to retrieve available options.

resource_type_name.description: Name of the resource type (machine size) assigned to this process

schedule.description: Cron expression defining the execution schedule. Only applicable to cron processes.

time_zone.description: IANA time zone for the cron schedule. Only applicable to cron processes.

job_start_policy.description: When the job process should run relative to deployments. `beforeDeployment` - run before the deployment starts. `afterSuccessDeployment` - run after a successful deployment. `afterFailedDeployment` - run after a failed deployment. Only applicable to job processes.

get.description:
Open an interactive terminal (shell) session to a running application process.

**This is a WebSocket endpoint.** Connect using a WebSocket client with the `Upgrade: websocket` header. Standard HTTP requests return a `426 Upgrade Required` response.

### Connection

```
wscat -c "wss://api.sevalla.com/v3/applications/{id}/processes/{process_id}/terminal?shell=bash" -H "Authorization: Bearer <api_key>"
```

Authentication runs before the WebSocket upgrade - invalid credentials are rejected with a standard HTTP 401/403 response.

### Communication

Once connected, the WebSocket streams stdin/stdout bidirectionally to a shell running inside the container. Send text messages as shell input and receive command output as text messages.

### Close codes

- `4000` - Application or process not found
- `4001` - No running pod available to connect to
- `4002` - Shell stream error
- `4003` - Shell stream closed normally
- `4005` - Failed to connect to the pod
- `4300` - Unexpected server error

parameters.name: shell
parameters.description: Shell to use for the terminal session. `sh` - Bourne shell (default). `bash` - Bourne Again Shell. `dash` - Debian Almquist Shell. `ksh` - KornShell. `zsh` - Z Shell. `csh` - C Shell. `tcsh` - TENEX C Shell.

get.description: Retrieve runtime logs for an application. These are logs produced by the running application processes (stdout/stderr). Use the `filters` parameter for advanced filtering by severity, message content, tags, or attributes.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the time range in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to 1 hour ago if not provided.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the time range in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to now if not provided.

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: JSON-encoded array of filter objects for advanced log filtering. Each filter has a `key`, `operator`, and `value` or `values` field. Example: [{"key":"severity","operator":"EQUALS","value":"ERROR"}]

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of log entries to return (1-5000)

id.description: Unique identifier for the log entry

service.description: Service that produced the log entry, such as APP_RUNTIME, APP_ACCESS, APP_DEPLOYMENT, SS_DEPLOYMENT, or SS_ACCESS

severity.description: Log severity level such as INFO, WARN, ERROR, or DEBUG

timestamp.description: Formatted timestamp of the log entry

tags.description: Key-value pairs of tags associated with the log entry, such as container name or resource ID

attributes.description: Additional structured attributes attached to the log entry

unix_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp of the log entry in microseconds

post.description: Create a new database on a specified cluster. The database is provisioned asynchronously - the initial status will be `creating` and will transition to `ready` once provisioning completes.

display_name.description: Human-readable name for the new database. Must be between 2 and 64 characters.

type.description: Database engine type. `postgresql` - PostgreSQL. `mariadb` - MariaDB. `mysql` - MySQL. `redis` - Redis. `valkey` - Valkey.

cluster_id.description: Identifier of the cluster (data center location) where the database will be created. Use the List Clusters endpoint to retrieve available options.

resource_type_id.description: Identifier of the database resource type (machine size). Use the List Database Resource Types endpoint to retrieve available options.

db_name.description: Name of the database to create within the server. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, underscores, hyphens, or plus signs.

db_password.description: Password for the database user. Must be between 4 and 100 characters and contain only alphanumeric characters and special characters (_?<>#@-=+).

db_user.description: Username for database authentication. Cannot be "root" or "postgres". If omitted, the database engine default user is used.

project_id.description: Identifier of the project to group this database under. When omitted, the database is not assigned to any project.

extensions.description: PostgreSQL extensions to enable. Only applicable when type is `postgresql`.

enable_vector.description: Enable the pgvector extension for vector similarity search

enable_postgis.description: Enable the PostGIS extension for geospatial data

enable_cron.description: Enable the pg_cron extension for scheduled jobs

company_id.description: Identifier of the company that owns this database

project_id.description: Identifier of the project this database is grouped under

name.description: Unique system name used in internal references. Auto-generated from the display name and cannot be changed after creation.

display_name.description: Human-readable name shown in the dashboard and API responses

type.description: Database engine type. `postgresql` - PostgreSQL. `mariadb` - MariaDB. `mysql` - MySQL. `redis` - Redis. `valkey` - Valkey.

status.description: Current status of the database reflecting the most recent lifecycle operation

is_suspended.description: Whether the database is currently suspended

resource_type_name.description: Name of the resource type (machine size) assigned to this database

created_at.description: Timestamp when the database was created, in ISO 8601 format

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the database was last modified, in ISO 8601 format

status.description: Current verification and pointing status of the domain. `VERIFICATION` - domain ownership is being verified. `POINTING` - domain is verified but DNS is not yet pointing to the platform. `ACTIVE` - domain is fully configured and serving traffic. `TIMED_OUT` - verification or pointing timed out and needs to be refreshed.

type.description: Domain type. `SYSTEM` - platform-assigned domain that can be toggled on/off. `CUSTOM` - user-added domain with custom DNS configuration.

is_enabled.description: Whether the domain is currently enabled. Only applicable to system domains.

is_wildcard.description: Whether this is a wildcard domain that matches all subdomains

is_primary.description: Whether this domain is the primary domain for the service. The primary domain is used for canonical URLs.

dns_records.description: DNS records that need to be configured for this domain. Null when DNS configuration is not required.

errors.description: Any errors encountered during domain verification or configuration

created_at.description: Timestamp when the domain was created, in ISO 8601 format

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the domain was last modified, in ISO 8601 format

get.description: Retrieve HTTP access logs for a static site. These are logs of incoming requests served by the static site. Use the `filters` parameter for advanced filtering by severity, message content, tags, or attributes.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the time range in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to 1 hour ago if not provided.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the time range in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to now if not provided.

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: JSON-encoded array of filter objects for advanced log filtering. Each filter has a `key`, `operator`, and `value` or `values` field. Example: [{"key":"severity","operator":"EQUALS","value":"ERROR"}]

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of log entries to return (1-5000)

id.description: Unique identifier for the log entry

service.description: Service that produced the log entry, such as APP_RUNTIME, APP_ACCESS, APP_DEPLOYMENT, SS_DEPLOYMENT, or SS_ACCESS

severity.description: Log severity level such as INFO, WARN, ERROR, or DEBUG

timestamp.description: Formatted timestamp of the log entry

tags.description: Key-value pairs of tags associated with the log entry, such as container name or resource ID

attributes.description: Additional structured attributes attached to the log entry

unix_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp of the log entry in microseconds

payload.description: JSON-encoded event payload that was sent to the endpoint

patch.description: Update the subdomain name of a system domain. Only applies to system domains - use the add/delete endpoints to manage custom domains.

name.description: New subdomain name for the system domain
Domain name, either a custom domain or a system-assigned subdomain

status.description: Current verification and pointing status of the domain. `VERIFICATION` - domain ownership is being verified. `POINTING` - domain is verified but DNS is not yet pointing to the platform. `ACTIVE` - domain is fully configured and serving traffic. `TIMED_OUT` - verification or pointing timed out and needs to be refreshed.

type.description: Domain type. `SYSTEM` - platform-assigned domain that can be toggled on/off. `CUSTOM` - user-added domain with custom DNS configuration.

is_enabled.description: Whether the domain is currently enabled. Only applicable to system domains.

is_wildcard.description: Whether this is a wildcard domain that matches all subdomains

is_primary.description: Whether this domain is the primary domain for the service. The primary domain is used for canonical URLs.

dns_records.description: DNS records that need to be configured for this domain. Null when DNS configuration is not required.

errors.description: Any errors encountered during domain verification or configuration

created_at.description: Timestamp when the domain was created, in ISO 8601 format

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the domain was last modified, in ISO 8601 format

post.description: Request a new device authorization code. The external client displays this code to the user, who then visits the Sevalla dashboard to approve the device. The client polls the GET endpoint until the code is approved, denied, or expired. Codes expire after 5 minutes. Maximum 5 active codes per IP address.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the device code expires. Codes are valid for 5 minutes

get.description: Check the current status of a device authorization code. The external client should poll this endpoint at regular intervals (recommended every 5 seconds) until the status changes from `pending`. When approved, the response includes the API key token for a one-time retrieval - the token cannot be fetched again.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The device code to check the status of

status.description: Current status of the device code. `pending` - waiting for user action, `approved` - user approved and token is included, `denied` - user explicitly rejected the request, `expired` - code has expired

token.description: The API key token, only present when status is `approved`. This is the only time the token is returned - store it securely
